senses.definition: to be forced to wait for a person or anticipate something

examples.x: I am cooling my heels in the waiting room until my name is called.
She cooled her heels for hours at the airport before her delayed flight finally departed.
They will have to cool their heels until the meeting starts.
He is currently cooling his heels outside the principal's office, waiting to discuss the incident.
They were cooling their heels in line at the amusement park while waiting for the ride to open.
By this time next week, we will still be cooling our heels in the queue for the concert tickets.

senses.definition: to allow or wait for a situation to become calmer or more stable after a significant change or serious dispute

examples.x: I'm going to let the dust settle before making a decision on the matter.
He let the dust settle after the argument before approaching his friend to reconcile.
They will let the dust settle before announcing any changes to the company structure.
We are currently letting the dust settle and observing the situation before taking any action.
She was letting the dust settle, allowing time for emotions to calm down, before addressing the issue.
By this time next year, they will still be letting the dust settle and analyzing the long-term impacts.

senses.definition: to patiently wait and avoid taking action until one finds a suitable or advantageous time

examples.x: I am biding my time, waiting for the perfect moment to present my idea.
He bided his time for years, patiently honing his skills before launching his successful business.
They will bide their time until the market conditions are favorable for their investment.
She is currently biding her time, gathering information and preparing for the right opportunity to arise.

senses.definition: to wait for something anxiously or eagerly

examples.x: We held our breath as the results were announced, hoping for a positive outcome.
They will be holding their breath during the performance, anticipating a breathtaking finale.
They were holding their breath throughout the entire negotiation process, unsure of the outcome.
I've emailed the company about my complaint, but don't hold your breath for a reply.
He said he'd be here in 15 minutes, but given his track record, I wouldn't hold my breath.

senses.definition: to hold on and not to rush into doing something, often used imperatively

examples.x: We need to hold our horses and carefully consider our options before proceeding.
Before making any decisions, it's important to hold your horses and gather all the necessary information.
He realized he needed to hold his horses and wait for the right moment to present his plan.
Hold her horses for a moment while she reviews the details of the contract.
They were about to jump into action, but I advised them to hold their horses and assess the risks first.

word_list.pronunciation: kˈiːp wˈʌnz ʃˈɜːt ɔːɹ pˈænts ˈɑːn

senses.definition: used for telling a person to be more patient or to think before reacting angrily

examples.x: Keep your pants on and let's discuss the issue calmly and rationally.
I told him to keep his shirt on during the argument, but he couldn't control his temper.
Tomorrow, remember to keep your pants on when dealing with difficult customers.
I know you're excited about the game, but keep your pants on.
She was about to yell at her colleague, but he told her to keep her shirt on and discuss the issue calmly.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt ðə ɡɹˈæs ɡɹˈoʊ ˌʌndɚ wˈʌnz fˈiːt

senses.definition: to spend time wastefully or do something with delay

examples.x: Don't let the grass grow under your feet—take action and pursue your goals now.
She didn't let the grass grow under her feet; she quickly seized the opportunity and made significant progress.
Tomorrow, I won't let the grass grow under my feet—I'll start working on my project right away.
He's not letting the grass grow under his feet; he's constantly seeking new opportunities and making strides in his career.
By this time next year, she will be avoiding letting the grass grow under her feet by consistently working towards her long-term goals.

senses.definition: to stop doing anything for a while
the assessment or understanding of the current state or conditions of a particular situation or context, specifically at a specific point or period

examples.x: I'm meeting with the team to discuss how the land lies regarding the project's progress.
Yesterday, we had a meeting to assess how the land lay after the changes in leadership.
